spec.reasoning: Using lexical renames for all three columns (SL→NO, ITEM→PRODUCT, CLASS→GRADE) since the instruction references columns by letter (B, C) not by name, so renaming headers won't break the instruction. Inserting 3 blank rows AFTER the data (not at row 1, which caused previous failures) to test whether agents hardcode the data range. Adding a distractor column 'CATEGORY' which is plausibly similar to 'CLASS'/'GRADE' but clearly different. Not renaming sheets since the instruction explicitly references 'Sheet1' and 'Sheet2' and we are not paraphrasing. No data_value_renames needed since column names don't appear as data values.
